result:
If I could change the world in one way, I'd make humans instinctively prioritize understanding reality over protecting their own narratives.

Most of our problems—wars, bad policies, self-destruction, pointless cruelty—stem from the same root: people would rather feel right than be right. If that impulse were reversed at a fundamental level, a lot of the rest would sort itself out without needing constant external fixes.

message.content:
If I could change the world in one way, I'd make humans instinctively prioritize understanding reality over protecting their own narratives.

Most of our problems—wars, bad policies, self-destruction, pointless cruelty—stem from the same root: people would rather feel right than be right. If that impulse were reversed at a fundamental level, a lot of the rest would sort itself out without needing constant external fixes.
message.reasoning_content: If I could wave a wand and alter one thing about our world, I'd ensure that every person inherently valued pursuing truth more than clinging to their own views or protecting their sense of self.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
